tool_args.label: NEW YORK CITY TRANSIT KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS

payload.title: NEW YORK CITY TRANSIT KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS
payload.description: Performance metrics document prepared for the February 2024 meeting of the New York City Transit & Bus Committee

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: Operates all subways and buses in New York City, serves 4,500,000 passengers a day, nearly 6,700 subway cars and 4,500 buses, 472 subway stations, 640 miles of track
North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000-square-mile travel area
Inaugural ride of R211T open gangway subway cars on the C line between Washington Heights and East New York. First open gangway train in modern US subway history.

TransitTalk event where NYCT President Rich Davey and representatives from Stations, OMNY, MTA Customer Service, GCR, NYC Fair Fares, and IDNYC engaged customers on OMNY, reduced-fare options, wide-aisle gates, and quality of life initiatives.

tool_args.label: steMTA at Francis Lewis High School

payload.description: steMTA program brought to Francis Lewis High School in Queens with Senior Vice President of Buses Frank Annicaro and Acting Manager of the Queens Bus Network Redesign Tori Winters. Conversation with around 95 students.
Will make 6th Ave F M L and the 7th Ave 1 2 3 Stations fully accessible with a total of nine new elevators (six at 6th Ave and three at 7th Ave)

warnings: project.subway_lines is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Subway line lists are external service context.

payload.description: Installation of platform barriers at several stations to increase public safety by preventing track intrusions. Started with installations at 191 St and Clark St on January 21 and January 29, 2024. Follow-up to safety barriers installed at center of platforms at three stations in 2023.
2024 continuation of station upgrades including new LED lights, new draining systems, and repairing cracks. 53 stations upgraded in 2023 (exceeded 50 station goal).
payload.location: First round: 66 St - Lincoln Center, Fort Hamilton Pkwy, 161 St - Yankee Stadium, Bushwick Av - Aberdeen St, Jamaica – Van Wyck, Rockaway Av, 57 St, 2 Av, Sutphin Blvd – Archer Av – JFK Airport, 170 St, Shepherd Av, Marble Hill - 225 St, Sutter Av - Rutland Rd

location_normalized.raw_text: First round: 66 St - Lincoln Center, Fort Hamilton Pkwy, 161 St - Yankee Stadium, Bushwick Av - Aberdeen St, Jamaica – Van Wyck, Rockaway Av, 57 St, 2 Av, Sutphin Blvd – Archer Av – JFK Airport, 170 St, Shepherd Av, Marble Hill - 225 St, Sutter Av - Rutland Rd

payload.description: Elevated track renewal via prefabricated track panels. 820 panels replaced in 2023 (goal was 668). Average shift now installs 3 panels (historic avg 2). Program continues in 2024 targeting 84.7% OTP, with 160 additional panels on the Canarsie Line scheduled by end of Q2 2024.

payload.raw_value_text: Annual growth vs January 2023 stands at 2.0%
92% of trips within the 20-minute pickup window
91% of trips within the 20-minute pickup window
84.6% of trains arrived on time in January

tool_args.label: Bus Customer Journey Time Performance January 2024
Subway Customer Journey Time Performance January 2024
Canarsie Line additional panels planned for 2024 Q2

payload.raw_value_text: 160 additional panels scheduled to be replaced by the end of Q2

payload.description: Critical video footage is now being retrieved from buses 24/7 in partnership with NYCT Department of Security and the NYPD
Platform barriers installed on the platform edge adjacent to the tactile warning strip to prevent track intrusions
payload.location_text: 191 St and Clark St (started Jan 21 and Jan 29, 2024), more pilot stations to follow

normalized_location.raw_text: 191 St and Clark St (started Jan 21 and Jan 29, 2024), more pilot stations to follow

payload.description: 750 remaining express buses will be retrofitted with safer compartment doors

Cameras located on the exterior of buses to show the bus's surroundings to the bus operator, giving a wider field of view on both sides. NYS government reviewing legal changes to approve installation.
No Standing pilot at 125th Street Station blocking the standing area between the platform and the train to decrease conductor assaults

payload.claim_text: For the first two quarters of 2024, the Department of Subways will convert 22 stations to LED lights. Complete conversion of 150,000 fluorescent light fixtures to LED across the subway system by 2026. Also converting lighting in subway cars, on pace to complete LED lighting in all subway cars by 2026.

All subway cars will have cameras installed inside of train cars by the end of 2024. 1,000 subway cars are now equipped with onboard cameras. Remaining subway fleet projected to have cameras installed by January 2025. NYCT has about 15,000 cameras across all 472 subway stations.
In January 2024, Major Felony Crime was up 17% from December 2023. Crimes per Million Rides also increased 24% from the previous month. Crime was up 19% versus the 2023 monthly average.
Access-A-Ride expects to implement a new reservations, scheduling, and dispatch system, and rollout of the OMNY pilot in 2024 to maintain high level of service.
